title: Cosmic Bloom
prompt:
Create a modern 160-second Nu Disco song for a web radio / web TV broadcast. 

Mood: Energetic, Uplifting, Euphoric.
Tempo: 120 BPM.
Style: Modern Nu Disco with a focus on driving basslines, shimmering synths, and subtle, contemporary production techniques.
Production: Clean, polished, radio-ready. Emphasis on wide stereo imaging and a dynamic mix. 
Instruments: Roland Juno-106, Yamaha DX7, LinnDrum, electric bass, funky clean electric guitar, layered vocal chops.

Structure:
0:00 - 0:12 Intro: Atmospheric synth pads and a subtle, pulsing bassline establish the groove. Minimal percussion enters.
0:12 - 0:44 Verse/Groove: Bassline becomes more prominent.  Funky clean electric guitar stabs add texture.  Subtle vocal chops start to weave in.
0:44 - 1:00 Build/Pre-Chorus:  Filter sweep on the synth pad. Percussion builds in intensity.  Layered vocal chops become more complex.
1:00 - 1:32 Chorus/Drop/Main Hook: Driving bassline and a powerful, syncopated drum beat. Layered vocal chops create a hypnotic effect. Bright synth arpeggios.
1:32 - 2:04 Second Verse or Soft Bridge: The energy slightly reduces. Electric guitar plays a melodic counter-melody. Vocal chops become more sparse.
2:04 - 2:32 Final Chorus/Drop/Main Hook:  Full energy return. Vocal chops are at their peak. Synth arpeggios are more complex. 
2:32 - 2:40 Final chorus continues with smooth fade out

Vocals:
Modern, chopped vocal samples used as textural elements, not for lyrical content. Focus on creating rhythmic patterns and adding sonic interest.

Lyrics:
N/A - Vocal chops only.

The final 8 seconds must fade out smoothly and naturally. No abrupt ending. No spoken outro.

Negative Prompt: low quality, distorted vocals, out of tune vocals, bad timing, messy mix, muddy bass, harsh highs, old-fashioned arrangement, theatrical singing, opera vocals, excessive vibrato, abrupt ending, spoken outro, crackle, noise, clipping, overcompressed, random tempo changes, long silence.
